conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s like watching someone else live my life. I look at people moving around, and I feel like I’m just stuck on the sidelines.
You mentioned feeling stuck on the sidelines—has that made it hard to care about things you used to enjoy?
Yeah, it has. I used to love reading, but now it feels pointless. I don't know why I'm wasting my time with it when I can't even enjoy it.
When you say reading feels pointless, does that feeling spill over into other parts of your life?
It does. Everything feels pointless, not just reading. I used to have a sense of purpose, but now I don't know what I'm fighting for.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It started maybe six months ago, when my divorce finalized. It took me a while to realize it, but I think that was the moment I lost my sense of direction.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
The quiet ones. Just being alone in the house. It's like I'm waiting for something to happen, but I know it won't.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I don't think I can keep doing this. I'm just going through the motions until I figure out what to do next.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
It feels like giving up, but it's also just being honest with myself. I'm not getting any younger, and I don't have the energy to keep trying to fix this.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
I don't think anyone would be worse off, but I don't think they'd notice either. It feels like I'm just a background noise that nobody needs.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it hard to care about stuff you normally enjoy during the day?
Yeah, it does. I used to love hiking, but now I just can't be bothered. It feels like too much effort to get out there and enjoy it when I’m just stuck in my head.
You mentioned thinking about things you could have done differently—what kinds of things come to mind?
Just the usual stuff. I keep replaying that breakup in my head, wondering if there was something I missed or if I should have done things differently.
When you think about that breakup now, how do you feel about yourself?
Like I’m the one who messed it up. I keep thinking maybe I was too much or maybe I wasn’t enough.
When you're feeling like you messed things up, does that make you feel more on edge or irritable with people around you?
Definitely. I get annoyed at my friends for moving on with their lives, and I know it’s not fair to them.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
About a month after the breakup. It was like the initial shock wore off, and then I just felt empty and stuck.
You mentioned feeling empty and stuck—what does that feel like on a typical day?
Just... nothing. I sit at my desk, and it feels like there’s a hole in my chest. I try to work, but I just can’t focus.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s like I’m just going through the motions. I used to be the kind of person who was always busy or planning something, but now I just feel like I’m just existing.
When you say this will end soon, are you talking about ending your life?
No, I just mean... eventually I’ll get over it. I have to, right? But right now, it feels like it’s going to take forever.
Have you thought about specific steps or a specific way you would do it?
No, I don't. I just think about how it would be easier to just stop it.
Do you currently have access to what you would use?
I have some pills left over from the breakup. I know it’s not the answer, but sometimes it feels like the only way to stop this pain.
